task.title: Saving search queries
task.shortdesc: You can save a Remote z/OS Search or Remote z/OS File Search query and run it from the Remote Systems view.

taskbody.prereq: Before you can save a search query, you must first run a remote z/OS search or remote z/OS file search and have the search results displayed in the Remote z/OS Search view. The search results must be limited to a single z/OS system. See the related topics for instructions.

steps.stepsection: To save a remote z/OS search or remote z/OS file search query:

step.cmd: In the Remote z/OS Search view, display the results of the search query you want to save.
step.info: If you completed more than one search, use the search history list to select the search query you want to save.

step.cmd: Click the Add query to My Favorites in Remote Systems view icon to save the search query.
step.info:
This icon is enabled only if the search scope is limited to a single z/OS system under the following conditions:
You search for files.
The search scope is for a single z/OS system. The scope can be an entire z/OS system or multiple partitioned data sets on the same system.
The search scope does not include z/OS projects or MVS subprojects. .
step.stepresult: The Add Query to Remote Systems View window opens.

step.cmd: Type a name for the query and click OK.
step.stepresult: The search query is added to the My Favorites folder of the MVS Files subsystem.

taskbody.postreq:
After you save a search query, you can do the following actions:
To run a saved query, select it in the Remote Systems view and click Run Query from the menu.
To edit and run a saved query, select it in the Remote Systems view and click Edit and Run from the menu. This action opens the Remote z/OS Search or Remote z/OS File Search window, which you can use to change the search criteria and submit the search request.

link.linktext: Quick task: Creating reusable z/OS search queries
